channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Kerry 2026 Taste Charts Highlight Changing African Food Preferences
item.description: Kerry, the global leader in taste and nutrition, imefanya launch ya Kerry 2026 taste charts, ikionyesha mabadiliko makubwa kwenye preferences za flavour za wateja barani Africa. Ripoti hizi zinashughu...

item.title: Congress Approves New Schedule for BARMM Elections; Comelec to Begin Preparations
item.description: The Office of the Presidential Adviser on Peace, Reconciliation and Unity (OPAPRU) has expressed support following Congress’ approval of bills postponing the first-ever Bangsamoro Autonomous Region in...

item.title: Memorable and Bold Fashion Moments on the Oscars Red Carpet
item.description: The Oscars are renowned for celebrating excellence in film, but the red carpet preceding the ceremony is a showcase of celebrity fashion daring and creativity. From Bjork's famous swan dress to Celine...

item.title: Federal government acts to stabilise fuel supply amid shortages
item.description: In response to ongoing panic buying and fuel shortages across Australia, the federal government has implemented additional measures to ensure domestic fuel supplies remain stable. The moves come as mo...

item.title: Kapsabet Girls Headgirl Speaks Confidently, President Ruto Donates KSh 1 Million for Students' Bread
item.description: During a visit to Kapsabet Girls High School on Thursday, March 12, President William Ruto was impressed by the school headgirl, Ombwogi Joy Kwamboka. The confident student walked up to the podium dur...

item.title: U.S. Ambassador Issues Apology to South African Government Over Undiplomatic Remarks
item.description: The United States Ambassador to South Africa, Brent Bozell, has formally apologised following remarks that were deemed undiplomatic after a meeting with officials from the Department of International ...

item.title: Atin Ito organizes humanitarian and civic mission to Pag-asa Island in April
item.description: Civil society coalition Atin Ito announced plans to conduct its fourth civilian mission to the West Philippine Sea this April, targeting Pag-asa Island in the Kalayaan Island Group. The initiative, ca...

item.title: John Lewis Partnership awards 2% bonus to staff after rise in profits
item.description: The John Lewis Partnership (JLP), the employee-owned retailer behind the department store chain and Waitrose supermarkets, has announced its first annual bonus since 2022. Staff, known as partners, wi...

item.title: Systemic Corruption and Negligence Plaguing Nigerian Public Universities
item.description: This opinion piece highlights serious wahala inside some Nigerian public universities, where students dey suffer because of missing exam scripts, compulsory 'sorting' (meaning lecturers demanding mone...

item.title: Tanzania na Canada Wapanga Kuimarisha Ushirikiano wa Kiuchumi na Mahusiano ya Kimaendeleo
item.description: Hapa London, Waziri wa Serikali katika Ofisi ya Rais (Kazi Maalum), Profesa Palamagamba Kabudi, alikutana na Naibu Waziri wa Mambo ya Nje wa Canada, Robert Oliphant, kujadili njia za kuimarisha ushiri...

item.title: DepEd: Friday WFH policy only for office-based non-teaching personnel
item.description: The Department of Education (DepEd) clarified that its work-from-home (WFH) policy for Fridays applies exclusively to non-teaching personnel working in DepEd offices. According to a memorandum dated M...

item.title: Mojtaba Khamenei, Iran's new Supreme Leader, reportedly hospitalised with serious injuries following airstrikes
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei, aged 56 and newly appointed Supreme Leader of Iran following the death of his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, is reported to be in a coma and has lost a leg after suffering serious i...

item.title: The Evolution and Enduring Legacy of the Polaroid Camera
item.description: The Polaroid camera, a symbol of instant photography, was born from a simple question asked by Edwin Land's three-year-old daughter in 1943: why couldn't she see her photo immediately? This curiosity ...

item.title: SSANU urges Federal Government to expedite renegotiation and address salary delays
item.description: The Senior Staff Association of Nigerian Universities (SSANU), University of Lagos (UNILAG) branch, don raise alarm say government dey neglect non-academic staff for Nigerian universities. According t...

item.title: Attacks on Schools in Conflict Zones Violate International Law
item.description: The bombing of the Shajareh Tayyebeh school in Minab, Iran, which reportedly killed 168 people, mostly schoolgirls, has shocked the international community. Carried out during class time, the attack r...

item.title: Madlanga Commission of Inquiry Continues Hearing Witnesses on Alleged Corruption
item.description: The Madlanga commission of inquiry, led by retired Justice Mbuyiseli Madlanga, resumed proceedings in Pretoria on Thursday. The commission is investigating allegations of criminal activity, political ...

item.title: NTA Reopens JEE Main 2026 Session 2 Registration Window Until 13th March
item.description: The National Testing Agency (NTA) has reopened the application window for candidates who wish to register for the JEE Main 2026 Session 2 examination. Students who missed the earlier registration peri...

item.title: Crocodile Sightings Surge in Northern Territory Floods: Understanding the Risk
item.description: Recent flooding in the Northern Territory, particularly around Katherine and the Daly River, has resulted in a notable increase in crocodile sightings. While the surge may seem alarming, it does not i...

item.title: UK pothole damage claims drop to record low as councils approve fewer compensations despite high reports
item.description: The number of successful compensation claims for vehicle damage caused by potholes fell to a record low in 2025, with councils across Britain approving only 19% of claims, down from 29% in 2024, accor...

item.title: Dane Cook’s career controversies and personal life challenges explained
item.description: Dane Cook, the American comedian, has had a career filled with ups and downs, affected by both professional controversies and personal decisions. He faced widespread criticism over allegations of joke...
